---
title: ""
pagetitle: "Shanshan Zhu"
page-layout: full
---

::: {.home-shell}
::: {.profile-card}
![](my_avatar.PNG){.profile-photo}

### Shanshan Zhu

M.Sc. Student in Psychology  
Nanjing Normal University

::: {.profile-actions}
[<i class="bi bi-file-earmark-person"></i> CV](cv.qmd)
[<i class="bi bi-journal-text"></i> Publications](publications.qmd)
:::

::: {.social-links}
[<i class="bi bi-envelope"></i> Email](mailto:zhushanshan0717@gmail.com)
[<i class="bi bi-github"></i> GitHub](https://github.com/shanshan717)
[<i class="bi bi-person-badge"></i> ORCID](https://orcid.org/0009-0007-6612-6190)
[<i class="bi bi-twitter-x"></i> Twitter](https://x.com/shansha18840596)
[<i class="bi bi-cloud-fill"></i> Bluesky](https://bsky.app/profile/zhushanshan0717.bsky.social)
:::
:::

::: {.main-content}
## About Me

I am a Master's student in Psychology at Nanjing Normal University. My research interests focus on self cognition, memory retrieval, and functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI).

My current work uses behavioral experiments, fMRI, and neuroimaging meta-analysis to understand how self-related information is represented in the brain.

In daily life, I enjoy running and strolling through parks. Although I am a slow-to-warm-up person, I am always happy to exchange ideas and share experiences with people who have similar interests.

::: {.interest-box}
#### Research Interests

- Self cognition
- Memory retrieval
- Functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI)
- Self-referential processing
- Neuroimaging meta-analysis
:::

<div class="section-heading">
  <h2 id="selected-publications">Selected Publications</h2>
  <a href="publications.qmd">View All <i class="bi bi-arrow-right"></i></a>
</div>

::: {.publication-list}
::: {.publication-card}
**Shared brain basis for altered self-referential processing across psychiatric disorders? A systematic review and meta-analysis of neuroimaging studies**

[**Shanshan Zhu**](https://orcid.org/0009-0007-6612-6190), Wenjin Yan, Chuan-Peng Hu  
*bioRxiv preprint*, 2026. doi: [10.64898/2026.03.13.711269](https://doi.org/10.64898/2026.03.13.711269)

This preprint synthesizes neuroimaging evidence on altered self-referential processing across psychiatric disorders. It has not yet been certified by peer review.

[<i class="bi bi-file-earmark-text"></i> bioRxiv](https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.64898/2026.03.13.711269v1){.publication-link}
:::
:::
:::
:::
